//note: I asked Gemini to give me 20 general moods and 5 specific moods for each (100 total) for this.
//: I then asked for it to give me coordinating color schemes for these moods which I have put in mood_colors.json

Hyped.Pumped: Super excited and ready to tackle a challenge.
Hyped.Thrilled: Highly pleased and buzzing over a specific win.
Hyped.Ecstatic: Overjoyed to the point of pure excitement.
Hyped.Amped: Filled with a sudden, high-voltage burst of energy.
Hyped.Eager: Impatiently waiting to get started on something fun.

Happy.Glad: Just feeling good about how things are going.
Happy.Cheerful: Bright, smiling, and in a naturally good mood.
Happy.Great: Feeling on top of things and highly positive.
Happy.Excited: Hyped up and looking forward to something.
Happy.Content: Totally satisfied and needing nothing else.

Energized.Energetic: Full of physical life and a desire to move around.
Energized.Bouncy: So full of energy you can't seem to sit still.
Energized.Restless: Fidgety and needing to find something to do.
Energized.Rowdy: Loud, playful, and bursting with active energy.

Proud.Accomplished: Satisfied after finishing a tough chore or goal.
Proud.Successful: Feeling like your hard work really paid off.
Proud.Confident: Sure of yourself and ready to stand tall.
Proud.Strong: Feeling emotionally or physically tough and resilient.
Proud.Validated: Feeling like your efforts were noticed and praised.

Smart.Focused: Concentrating heavily on a single task or goal.
Smart.Sharp: Thinking quickly and processing things fast.
Smart.Capable: Feeling like you have the exact skills to handle life.
Smart.Bright: Feeling clever, alert, and mentally quick.
Smart.Clever: Finding quick, witty ways to figure out a problem.

Loving.Cared-For: Feeling deeply valued, wanted, and protected.
Loving.Affectionate: Wanting to show warmth, hugs, or kind words.
Loving.Close: Feeling a strong, tight connection to someone.
Loving.Warm: A gentle, fuzzy feeling of emotional comfort.
Loving.Adoring: Filled with deep admiration and fondness.

Helpful.Kind: Feeling warm, friendly, and generous toward others.
Helpful.Caring: Deeply concerned with making sure people are okay.
Helpful.Friendly: Open, welcoming, and easy to talk to.
Helpful.Supportive: Ready to back someone up and help them out.
Helpful.Generous: Happy to share your time, energy, or things.

Hopeful.Optimistic: Confident that things will turn out well.
Hopeful.Encouraged: Feeling better about a situation after a small win.
Hopeful.Eager: Looking toward tomorrow with a positive attitude.
Hopeful.Inspired: Feeling a spark of desire to try something new.
Hopeful.Reassured: Feeling a sense of relief that everything will be fine.

Chill.Relaxed: Free from any immediate stress or pressure.
Chill.Lazy: Wanting to just lounge around and do nothing.
Chill.Mellow: Soft, low-key, and moving at a slow pace.
Chill.Comfortable: Perfectly at ease in your current space.

Good.Decent: Things are going fine, nothing to complain about.
Good.Okay: A baseline, neutral feeling where life is steady.
Good.Fine: Comfortable and structurally solid, if a bit ordinary.
Good.Alright: Feeling safe, stable, and reasonably well.
Good.Safe: Feeling secure and free from any threats or worries.

Bored.Dull: Life feels completely uninteresting and gray right now.
Bored.Listless: Lacking any desire, energy, or interest to do stuff.
Bored.Apathetic: Feeling a total shrug of "I don't care" about everything.
Bored.Restless: Bored but possessing too much energy to sit still.
Bored.Blah: Feeling entirely flat, empty, and uninspired.

Tired.Sleepy: Heavy-eyed and ready to crash in bed immediately.
Tired.Exhausted: Drained of all physical and mental battery.
Tired.Sluggish: Moving, thinking, and reacting way slower than usual.
Tired.Drained: Feeling like your energy was completely sucked away.
Tired.Worn-Out: Feeling broken down by a long day or hard work.

Confused.Lost: Having absolutely no idea what to do next.
Confused.Stuck: Blocked by a problem you can't find an answer to.
Confused.Foggy: Experiencing a mental haze that makes it hard to think.
Confused.Flustered: Becoming messy and chaotic under sudden pressure.
Confused.Mixed-Up: Getting facts, feelings, or details completely jumbled.

Lonely.Isolated: Feeling completely cut off and alone in the world.
Lonely.Left-Out: Excluded from the fun things your peers are doing.
Lonely.Forgotten: Feeling like nobody is checking in or thinking of you.
Lonely.Disconnected: Sitting in a room with people but feeling zero bond.
Lonely.Homesick: Missing familiar places, comfort, and family routines.

Annoyed.Irritated: Losing your patience over a small, repeating nuisance.
Annoyed.Frustrated: Stuck and upset because you can't fix a problem.
Annoyed.Bothered: Feeling a persistent, nagging sense of discomfort.
Annoyed.Snappy: Easily provoked into answering back sharply.

Worried.Nervous: Anxious, shaky, and highly tense about an event.
Worried.Anxious: A lingering, uneasy dread about what might happen.
Worried.Uneasy: Lacking comfort or a sense of safety.
Worried.Jittery: Shaky and totally on edge from tight nerves.
Worried.On-Edge: Highly sensitive to noise and easily stressed out.

Hurt.Crushed: Deeply wounded by harsh words or rejection.
Hurt.Bitter: Resentful because a situation felt incredibly unfair.
Hurt.Jealous: Hurt and envious of what someone else has.

Scared.Afraid: Feeling an immediate sense of danger or fright.
Scared.Terrified: Completely frozen or panicked by intense fear.
Scared.Panicky: Hit by sudden, overwhelming, and blinding worry.
Scared.Spooked: Easily startled and jumped by small surprises.

Sad.Down: Just feeling low, heavy, and unmotivated.
Sad.Gloomy: Feeling dark, rainy, and low-energy inside.
Sad.Heartbroken: Crushed by a big disappointment or loss.
Sad.Glum: Quietly miserable and not wanting to talk.
Sad.Bummed: Disappointed because something didn't go your way.

Mad.Angry: Filled with sudden, sharp irritation or fury.
Mad.Resentful: Highly annoyed and actively upset with a situation.
Mad.Furious: Boiling over with intense, high-energy rage.
Mad.Bitter: Holding onto old grudges and feeling sharp anger.
Mad.Hostile: Feeling an intense, deep dislike for a situation.
